title: Cephaelis muscosa
taxonomy: Cephaelis muscosa is classified within the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. It belongs to the genus Cephaelis, a group of flowering plants native to Central and South America. The classification of Cephaelis muscosa is further supported by its botanical name, which is a combination of its genus and species names. The genus Cephaelis is itself part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as the angiosperms. Cephaelis muscosa is therefore a member of the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Cephaelis muscosa, a plant species, is traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and stems are often employed to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant is said to possess antimalarial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its healing effects. In traditional medicine, Cephaelis muscosa is also used to treat digestive problems and wounds. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cephaelis muscosa for medicinal purposes.

intro: Cephaelis muscosa, commonly known as the Mayan cocoa tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Central America, to treat various health conditions, such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cephaelis muscosa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional remedies, including cough syrups and digestive tonics.
